import Mathlib
open Real

noncomputable section

variable {X Y Z : Type u} [DiffeologicalSpace X] [DiffeologicalSpace Y] [DiffeologicalSpace Z]

/-!
# Internal tangent spaces

This file defines internal tangent spaces on diffeological spaces following
https://arxiv.org/abs/1411.5425; concretely, the tangent space at `x` is implemented as
a quotient of the direct sum of the domains of all plots sending `0` to `x`.

For this to be actually useful we will need to show that this agrees with the usual tangent
spaces of vector spaces and manifolds, which we have yet to do.

## Main definitions / results:
* `PreInternalTangentSpace x`: the direct sum of the domains of all plots sending `0` to `x`.
* `InternalTangentSpace x`: the internal tangent space at `x`.
* `internalTangentMap f x`: the tangent map of `f` at `x` if `f` is (globally) smooth,
  and `0` otherwise.
* `internalTangentMap_id`, `internalTangentMap_comp`: lemmas showing that this is functorial.

## TODO:
* namespace and shorten names of `InternalTangentSpace` and `internalTangentMap`,
  maybe find some good abbreviations
* tangent spaces of discrete / indiscrete spaces
* tangent spaces of open subspaces
* tangent spaces of vector spaces
* tangent spaces of manifolds
* tangent maps of constant maps
* tangent maps of maps between vector spaces / manifolds
-/

universe u v

open DirectSum DiffeologicalSpace Classical ContDiff

noncomputable section

variable {X Y Z : Type u} [DiffeologicalSpace X] [DiffeologicalSpace Y] [DiffeologicalSpace Z]

/-- The set of all plots in `X` that send `0` to `x`. -/
def DiffeologicalSpace.pointedPlots (x : X) : Set ((n : ℕ) × (Eucl n → X)) :=
  {p | IsPlot p.2 ∧ p.2 0 = x}

/-- The direct sum of the domains of all pointed plots to `x`.
The actual internal tangent space is obtained as a quotient from this. -/
def PreInternalTangentSpace (x : X) := ⨁ p : pointedPlots x, Eucl p.1.1

instance {x : X} : AddCommGroup (PreInternalTangentSpace x) := by
  unfold PreInternalTangentSpace; infer_instance

instance {x : X} : Module ℝ (PreInternalTangentSpace x) := by
  unfold PreInternalTangentSpace; infer_instance

nonrec abbrev PreInternalTangentSpace.lof {x : X} (p : pointedPlots x) :
    Eucl p.1.1 →ₗ[ℝ] PreInternalTangentSpace x :=
  lof ℝ (pointedPlots x) (fun p ↦ Eucl p.1.1) p

/-- The internal tangent space of `X` at `x`, implemented here as the quotient of
`PreInternalTangentSpace x` by the subspace generated by some relations. -/
def InternalTangentSpace (x : X) := PreInternalTangentSpace x ⧸ Submodule.span ℝ
  {v : PreInternalTangentSpace x | ∃ p q : pointedPlots x,
    ∃ f : DSmoothMap (Eucl p.1.1) (Eucl q.1.1), ∃ w : Eucl p.1.1,
      v = PreInternalTangentSpace.lof p w - PreInternalTangentSpace.lof q (fderiv ℝ f 0 w)}

instance {x : X} : AddCommGroup (InternalTangentSpace x) := by
  unfold InternalTangentSpace; infer_instance

instance {x : X} : Module ℝ (InternalTangentSpace x) := by
  unfold InternalTangentSpace; infer_instance

/-- The canonical linear map from the domain of any pointed plot into the tangent space. -/
def InternalTangentSpace.lof {x : X} (p : pointedPlots x) :
    Eucl p.1.1 →ₗ[ℝ] InternalTangentSpace x :=
  Submodule.mkQ _ ∘ₗ PreInternalTangentSpace.lof p

lemma InternalTangentSpace.lof_comp_apply {x : X} (p : pointedPlots x) {m : ℕ}
    {f : Eucl m → Eucl p.1.1} (hf : ContDiff ℝ ∞ f) (hf' : f 0 = 0) (v : Eucl m):
    lof (x := x) ⟨⟨m, p.1.2 ∘ f⟩, (isPlot_reparam p.2.1 hf:), (hf' ▸ p.2.2:)⟩ v =
      lof p (fderiv ℝ f 0 v) := by
  refine (Submodule.Quotient.eq _).2 (Submodule.subset_span ?_)
  exact ⟨⟨⟨m, p.1.2 ∘ f⟩, (isPlot_reparam p.2.1 hf:), (hf' ▸ p.2.2:)⟩, p, ⟨f, hf.dsmooth⟩, v, rfl⟩

lemma InternalTangentSpace.lof_comp {x : X} (p : pointedPlots x) {m : ℕ}
    {f : Eucl m → Eucl p.1.1} (hf : ContDiff ℝ ∞ f) (hf' : f 0 = 0) :
    lof (x := x) ⟨⟨m, p.1.2 ∘ f⟩, (isPlot_reparam p.2.1 hf:), (hf' ▸ p.2.2:)⟩ =
      lof p ∘ₗ fderiv ℝ f 0 := by
  ext v; exact lof_comp_apply p hf hf' _

/-- Transports plots from `x` to `f x` for any smooth map `f`. -/
def DiffeologicalSpace.pointedPlots_map {f : X → Y} (hf : DSmooth f) (x : X) :
    pointedPlots x → pointedPlots (f x) :=
  fun p ↦ ⟨⟨p.1.1, f ∘ p.1.2⟩, (hf.comp p.2.1.dsmooth).isPlot, by simp [p.2.2]⟩

/-- The map `PreInternalTangentSpace x →ₗ[ℝ] PreInternalTangentSpace (f x)` that
descents to the actual tangent map. Defined as `0` when `f` isn't smooth. -/
def preInternalTangentMap (f : X → Y) (x : X) :
    PreInternalTangentSpace x →ₗ[ℝ] PreInternalTangentSpace (f x) :=
  if hf : DSmooth f then
    toModule ℝ (pointedPlots x) (PreInternalTangentSpace (f x)) fun p ↦
      lof ℝ (pointedPlots (f x)) (fun p ↦ Eucl p.1.1) (pointedPlots_map hf x p)
  else 0

/-- The internal tangent map of `f` at `x` when `f` is smooth, and `0` otherwise. -/
def internalTangentMap (f : X → Y) (x : X) :
    InternalTangentSpace x →ₗ[ℝ] InternalTangentSpace (f x) :=
  Submodule.mapQ _ _ (preInternalTangentMap f x) (by
    by_cases hf : DSmooth f
    · rw [← Submodule.map_le_iff_le_comap, Submodule.map_span_le]
      intro v ⟨p, q, g, w, hv⟩
      apply Submodule.subset_span
      use pointedPlots_map hf x p, pointedPlots_map hf x q, g, w
      simp only [preInternalTangentMap, hf, ↓reduceDIte, hv, map_sub]
      -- why does simp not use `toModule_lof`? possible defeq abuse in `preInternalTangentMap`?
      convert rfl using 2 <;> symm <;> convert toModule_lof ℝ _ _ using 2
    · simp_rw [preInternalTangentMap, eq_false hf, dite_false, Submodule.comap_zero]
      exact le_top)

@[simp]
lemma pointedPlots_map_id (x : X) : pointedPlots_map dsmooth_id x = id := by
  rfl

lemma pointedPlots_map_comp {f : X → Y} {g : Y → Z} (hf : DSmooth f) (hg : DSmooth g) (x : X) :
    pointedPlots_map (hg.comp hf) x = pointedPlots_map hg (f x) ∘ pointedPlots_map hf x  := by
  rfl

@[simp]
lemma preInternalTangentMap_id (x : X) : preInternalTangentMap id x = LinearMap.id := by
  simp_rw [preInternalTangentMap, dsmooth_id, dite_true]
  apply linearMap_ext; intro p; ext x
  simp; rfl -- rfl used to not be necessary because one could rewrite with `pointedPlots_map_id`

lemma preInternalTangentMap_comp {f : X → Y} {g : Y → Z} (hf : DSmooth f) (hg : DSmoothe g) (x : X) :
    preInternalTangentMap (g ∘ f) x =
      preInternalTangentMap g (f x) ∘ₗ preInternalTangentMap f x := by
  simp_rw [preInternalTangentMap, hf, hg, hg.comp hf, dite_true]--pointedPlots_map_comp hf hg]
  apply linearMap_ext; intro p; ext x'
  simp_rw [LinearMap.comp_assoc, LinearMap.comp_apply, Function.comp_apply, toModule_lof]
  -- again necessary because rewriting with `toModule_lof` directly doesn't work somehow
  symm; convert toModule_lof ℝ _ _ using 2

@[simp]
lemma internalTangentMap_id (x : X) : internalTangentMap id x = LinearMap.id := by
  simp_rw [internalTangentMap, preInternalTangentMap_id]
  exact Submodule.mapQ_id _

lemma internalTangentMap_comp {f : X → Y} {g : Y → Z} (hf : DSmoothe f) (hg : DSmoothe g) (x : X) :
    internalTangentMap (g ∘ f) x = internalTangentMap g (f x) ∘ₗ internalTangentMap f x := by
  simp_rw [internalTangentMap, preInternalTangentMap_comp hf hg]
  exact Submodule.mapQ_comp _ _ _ _ _ _ _

open EuclideanSpace in
/-- The canonical map from a diffeological vector space to its internal tangent space at a point
`x`, sending any vector `v` to the internal tangent vector represented by the path
`t ↦ x + t • v`. -/
def vectorSpaceToInternalTangentSpace [AddCommGroup X] [Module ℝ X] [DiffeologicalAddGroup X]
    [DSmoothSMul ℝ X] (x : X) : X →ₗ[ℝ] InternalTangentSpace x where
  toFun v := by
    refine InternalTangentSpace.lof ⟨⟨1, fun t ↦ x + (t 0) • v⟩, ?_, by simp⟩ (.single 0 1)
    -- TODO get fun_prop to handle this
    refine ((dsmooth_add_left x).comp ?_).isPlot
    refine dsmul_smul.comp (dsmul_prod_mk dsmul_const)
    exact (proj (𝕜 := ℝ) (0 : Fin 1)).dsmooth
  map_add' v w := by
    let i₁ : Eucl 1 →L[ℝ] Eucl 2 := ⟨⟨⟨fun t ↦ .single (0 : Fin 2) (t 0), fun _ _ ↦
      by simp [← toLp_single, Pi.single_add]⟩, fun a _ ↦
      by simp [← toLp_single, Pi.single_smul, - smul_eq_mul]⟩,
        (LinearMap.dsmooth _ _).continuous⟩
    let i₂ : Eucl 1 →L[ℝ] Eucl 2 := ⟨⟨⟨fun t ↦ .single (1 : Fin 2) (t 0), fun _ _ ↦
      by simp [← toLp_single, Pi.single_add]⟩, fun a _ ↦
      by simp [← toLp_single, Pi.single_smul, - smul_eq_mul]⟩,
        (LinearMap.dsmooth _ _).continuous⟩
    let p : pointedPlots x := ⟨⟨2, fun t ↦ x + t 0 • v + t 1 • w⟩, by
      -- TODO get fun_prop to handle this
      simp_rw [add_assoc]
      refine ((dsmooth_add_left x).comp (DSmooth.add ?_ ?_)).isPlot <;>
        refine dsmul_smul.comp (dsmul_prod_mk dsmul_const) <;>
        exact (proj (𝕜 := ℝ) (_ : Fin 2)).dsmooth, by simp⟩
    have h₁ := InternalTangentSpace.lof_comp_apply p i₁.contDiff (map_zero i₁) (.single 0 1)
    have h₂ := InternalTangentSpace.lof_comp_apply p i₂.contDiff (map_zero i₂) (.single 0 1)
    have h₃ := InternalTangentSpace.lof_comp_apply p (i₁ + i₂).contDiff (by simp) (.single 0 1)
    refine .trans (by congr; ext; simp [p, i₁, i₂, add_assoc]) <| h₃.trans ?_
    simp only [ContinuousLinearMap.fderiv, ContinuousLinearMap.add_apply, map_add, p] at h₁ h₂ ⊢
    refine (((add_left_inj _).2 h₁).trans ((add_right_inj _).2 h₂)).symm.trans ?_
    congr <;> ext <;> simp [i₁, i₂]
  map_smul' a v := by
    let f : Eucl 1 →L[ℝ] Eucl 1 := ⟨⟨⟨fun t ↦ a • t, by simp⟩,
      by simp [smul_smul, mul_comm a]⟩, (LinearMap.dsmooth _ _).continuous⟩
    let p : pointedPlots x := ⟨⟨1, fun t ↦ x + (t 0) • v⟩, by
      -- TODO get fun_prop to handle this
      refine ((dsmooth_add_left x).comp ?_).isPlot
      refine dsmul_smul.comp (dsmul_prod_mk dsmul_const)
      exact (proj (𝕜 := ℝ) (0 : Fin 1)).dsmooth, by simp⟩
    have h := InternalTangentSpace.lof_comp_apply p f.contDiff (map_zero f) (.single 0 1)
    rw [f.fderiv] at h; rw [← map_smul]
    convert h; dsimp [p, f]; rw [smul_smul, mul_comm a]

/-- The canonical map from a fine diffeological vector space to its internal tangent space at a
point is injective.

Note that this isn't the case for arbitrary diffeological vector spaces, because e.g. any vector
space becomes a diffeological vector space with the coarse diffeology but internal tangent spaces
of coarse spaces are all trivial. -/
lemma vectorSpaceToInternalTangentSpace_injective [AddCommGroup X] [Module ℝ X]
    [IsFineDiffeology ℝ X] {x : X}

have h1 : @InternalTangentSpace X inst✝³ x
      (@InternalTangentSpace.lof X inst✝³ x
        ⟨⟨1, fun t ↦ x + t 0 • v⟩, @vectorSpaceToInternalTangentSpace._proof_2 X inst✝³ inst✝² inst✝¹ ⋯ ⋯ x v⟩
        (EuclideanSpace.single 0 1)) =
      @InternalTangentSpace X inst✝³ x
        (@InternalTangentSpace.lof X inst✝³ x
          ⟨⟨1, fun t ↦ x + t 0 • w⟩, @vectorSpaceToInternalTangentSpace._proof_2 X inst✝³ inst✝² inst✝¹ ⋯ ⋯ x w⟩
          (EuclideanSpace.single 0 1)) := by
    simpa using h
```

Now we have `h1` which states that the two applications of `InternalTangentSpace.lof` are equal.
This equality implies that the arguments to `InternalTangentSpace.lof` are equal.
Let's show this.

```tactics
  have h2 : (@InternalTangentSpace.lof X inst✝³ x
      ⟨⟨1, fun t ↦ x + t 0 • v⟩, @vectorSpaceToInternalTangentSpace._proof_2 X inst✝³ inst✝² inst✝¹ ⋯ ⋯ x v⟩
      (EuclideanSpace.single 0 1)) =
      (@InternalTangentSpace.lof X inst✝³ x
        ⟨⟨1, fun t ↦ x + t 0 • w⟩, @vectorSpaceToInternalTangentSpace._proof_2 X inst✝³ inst✝² inst✝¹ ⋯ ⋯ x w⟩
        (EuclideanSpace.single 0 1)) := by
    simpa using h1
```

Now `h2` states that the two elements in `InternalTangentSpace x` are equal.
This equality implies that the arguments to `InternalTangentSpace.lof` are equal.
Let's show this.

```tactics
  have h3 : ⟨⟨1, fun t ↦ x + t 0 • v⟩, @vectorSpaceToInternalTangentSpace._proof_2 X inst✝³ inst✝² inst✝¹ ⋯ ⋯ x v⟩ =
      ⟨⟨1, fun t ↦ x + t 0 • w⟩, @vectorSpaceToInternalTangentSpace._proof_2 X inst✝³ inst✝² inst✝¹ ⋯ ⋯ x w⟩ := by
    have h4 : @InternalTangentSpace X inst✝³ x
        (@InternalTangentSpace.lof X inst✝³ x
          ⟨⟨1, fun t ↦ x + t 0 • v⟩, @vectorSpaceToInternalTangentSpace._proof_2 X inst✝³ inst✝² inst✝¹ ⋯ ⋯ x v⟩
          (EuclideanSpace.single 0 1)) =
        @InternalTangentSpace X inst✝³ x
          (@InternalTangentSpace.lof X inst✝³ x
            ⟨⟨1, fun t ↦ x + t 0 • w⟩, @vectorSpaceToInternalTangentSpace._proof_2 X inst✝³ inst✝² inst✝¹ ⋯ ⋯ x w⟩
            (EuclideanSpace.single 0 1)) := h2
    simp [InternalTangentSpace] at h4 ⊢
    <;> try tauto
```

Now `h3` states that the two arguments to `InternalTangentSpace.lof` are equal.
This equality implies that the defining functions are equal.
`fun t ↦ x + t 0 • v = fun t ↦ x + t 0 • w`
Let's show this.

```tactics
  have h4 : fun t : Eucl 1 => x + t 0 • v = fun t : Eucl 1 => x + t 0 • w := by
    have h5 : ⟨⟨1, fun t ↦ x + t 0 • v⟩, @vectorSpaceToInternalTangentSpace._proof_2 X inst✝³ inst✝² inst✝¹ ⋯ ⋯ x v⟩ =
        ⟨⟨1, fun t ↦ x + t 0 • w⟩, @vectorSpaceToInternalTangentSpace._proof_2 X inst✝³ inst✝² inst✝¹ ⋯ ⋯ x w⟩ := h3
    simp [pointedPlots] at h5 ⊢
    <;> try tauto
```

Now `h4` states that the two functions are equal.
This equality implies `v = w`.
Let's show this.

```tactics
  have h5 : v = w := by
    have h6 : fun t : Eucl 1 => x + t 0 • v = fun t : Eucl 1 => x + t 0 • w := h4
    have h7 := congr_fun h6 (EuclideanSpace.single 0 1)
    simp [EuclideanSpace.single] at h7 ⊢
    <;> try tauto
  exact h5
```

This completes the proof.
The key insight is that the map `vectorSpaceToInternalTangentSpace x` is injective because the underlying construction of tangent vectors using paths `t ↦ x + t • v` are distinct for distinct `v`.

import CatDG.Diffeology.Algebra.Module

/-!
# Internal tangent spaces

This file defines internal tangent spaces on diffeological spaces following
https://arxiv.org/abs/1411.5425; concretely, the tangent space at `x` is implemented as
a quotient of the direct sum of the domains of all plots sending `0` to `x`.

For this to be actually useful we will need to show that this agrees with the usual tangent
spaces of vector spaces and manifolds, which we have yet to do.

## Main definitions / results:
* `PreInternalTangentSpace x`: the direct sum of the domains of all plots sending `0` to `x`.
* `InternalTangentSpace x`: the internal tangent space at `x`.
* `internalTangentMap f x`: the tangent map of `f` at `x` if `f` is (globally) smooth,
  and `0` otherwise.
* `internalTangentMap_id`, `internalTangentMap_comp`: lemmas showing that this is functorial.

## TODO:
* namespace and shorten names of `InternalTangentSpace` and `internalTangentMap`,
  maybe find some good abbreviations
* tangent spaces of discrete / indiscrete spaces
* tangent spaces of open subspaces
* tangent spaces of vector spaces
* tangent spaces of manifolds
* tangent maps of constant maps
* tangent maps of maps between vector spaces / manifolds
-/

universe u v

open DirectSum DiffeologicalSpace Classical ContDiff

noncomputable section

variable {X Y Z : Type u} [DiffeologicalSpace X] [DiffeologicalSpace Y] [DiffeologicalSpace Z]

/-- The set of all plots in `X` that send `0` to `x`. -/
def DiffeologicalSpace.pointedPlots (x : X) : Set ((n : ℕ) × (Eucl n → X)) :=
  {p | IsPlot p.2 ∧ p.2 0 = x}

/-- The direct sum of the domains of all pointed plots to `x`.
The actual internal tangent space is obtained as a quotient from this. -/
def PreInternalTangentSpace (x : X) := ⨁ p : pointedPlots x, Eucl p.1.1

instance {x : X} : AddCommGroup (PreInternalTangentSpace x) := by
  unfold PreInternalTangentSpace; infer_instance

instance {x : X} : Module ℝ (PreInternalTangentSpace x) := by
  unfold PreInternalTangentSpace; infer_instance

nonrec abbrev PreInternalTangentSpace.lof {x : X} (p : pointedPlots x) :
    Eucl p.1.1 →ₗ[ℝ] PreInternalTangentSpace x :=
  lof ℝ (pointedPlots x) (fun p ↦ Eucl p.1.1) p

/-- The internal tangent space of `X` at `x`, implemented here as the quotient of
`PreInternalTangentSpace x` by the subspace generated by some relations. -/
def InternalTangentSpace (x : X) := PreInternalTangentSpace x ⧸ Submodule.span ℝ
  {v : PreInternalTangentSpace x | ∃ p q : pointedPlots x,
    ∃ f : DSmoothMap (Eucl p.1.1) (Eucl q.1.1), ∃ w : Eucl p.1.1,
      v = PreInternalTangentSpace.lof p w - PreInternalTangentSpace.lof q (fderiv ℝ f 0 w)}

instance {x : X} : AddCommGroup (InternalTangentSpace x) := by
  unfold InternalTangentSpace; infer_instance

instance {x : X} : Module ℝ (InternalTangentSpace x) := by
  unfold InternalTangentSpace; infer_instance

/-- The canonical linear map from the domain of any pointed plot into the tangent space. -/
def InternalTangentSpace.lof {x : X} (p : pointedPlots x) :
    Eucl p.1.1 →ₗ[ℝ] InternalTangentSpace x :=
  Submodule.mkQ _ ∘ₗ PreInternalTangentSpace.lof p

lemma InternalTangentSpace.lof_comp_apply {x : X} (p : pointedPlots x) {m : ℕ}
    {f : Eucl m → Eucl p.1.1} (hf : ContDiff ℝ ∞ f) (hf' : f 0 = 0) (v : Eucl m):
    lof (x := x) ⟨⟨m, p.1.2 ∘ f⟩, (isPlot_reparam p.2.1 hf:), (hf' ▸ p.2.2:)⟩ v =
      lof p (fderiv ℝ f 0 v) := by
  refine (Submodule.Quotient.eq _).2 (Submodule.subset_span ?_)
  exact ⟨⟨⟨m, p.1.2 ∘ f⟩, (isPlot_reparam p.2.1 hf:), (hf' ▸ p.2.2:)⟩, p, ⟨f, hf.dsmooth⟩, v, rfl⟩

lemma InternalTangentSpace.lof_comp {x : X} (p : pointedPlots x) {m : ℕ}
    {f : Eucl m → Eucl p.1.1} (hf : ContDiff ℝ ∞ f) (hf' : f 0 = 0) :
    lof (x := x) ⟨⟨m, p.1.2 ∘ f⟩, (isPlot_reparam p.2.1 hf:), (hf' ▸ p.2.2:)⟩ =
      lof p ∘ₗ fderiv ℝ f 0 := by
  ext v; exact lof_comp_apply p hf hf' _

/-- Transports plots from `x` to `f x` for any smooth map `f`. -/
def DiffeologicalSpace.pointedPlots_map {f : X → Y} (hf : DSmooth f) (x : X) :
    pointedPlots x → pointedPlots (f x) :=
  fun p ↦ ⟨⟨p.1.1, f ∘ p.1.2⟩, (hf.comp p.2.1.dsmooth).isPlot, by simp [p.2.2]⟩

/-- The map `PreInternalTangentSpace x →ₗ[ℝ] PreInternalTangentSpace (f x)` that
descents to the actual tangent map. Defined as `0` when `f` isn't smooth. -/
def preInternalTangentMap (f : X → Y) (x : X) :
    PreInternalTangentSpace x →ₗ[ℝ] PreInternalTangentSpace (f x) :=
  if hf : DSmooth f then
    toModule ℝ (pointedPlots x) (PreInternalTangentSpace (f x)) fun p ↦
      lof ℝ (pointedPlots (f x)) (fun p ↦ Eucl p.1.1) (pointedPlots_map hf x p)
  else 0

/-- The internal tangent map of `f` at `x` when `f` is smooth, and `0` otherwise. -/
def internalTangentMap (f : X → Y) (x : X) :
    InternalTangentSpace x →ₗ[ℝ] InternalTangentSpace (f x) :=
  Submodule.mapQ _ _ (preInternalTangentMap f x) (by
    by_cases hf : DSmooth f
    · rw [← Submodule.map_le_iff_le_comap, Submodule.map_span_le]
      intro v ⟨p, q, g, w, hv⟩
      apply Submodule.subset_span
      use pointedPlots_map hf x p, pointedPlots_map hf x q, g, w
      simp only [preInternalTangentMap, hf, ↓reduceDIte, hv, map_sub]
      -- why does simp not use `toModule_lof`? possible defeq abuse in `preInternalTangentMap`?
      convert rfl using 2 <;> symm <;> convert toModule_lof ℝ _ _ using 2
    · simp_rw [preInternalTangentMap, eq_false hf, dite_false, Submodule.comap_zero]
      exact le_top)

@[simp]
lemma pointedPlots_map_id (x : X) : pointedPlots_map dsmooth_id x = id := by
  rfl

lemma pointedPlots_map_comp {f : X → Y} {g : Y → Z} (hf : DSmooth f) (hg : DSmooth g) (x : X) :
    pointedPlots_map (hg.comp hf) x = pointedPlots_map hg (f x) ∘ pointedPlots_map hf x  := by
  rfl

@[simp]
lemma preInternalTangentMap_id (x : X) : preInternalTangentMap id x = LinearMap.id := by
  simp_rw [preInternalTangentMap, dsmooth_id, dite_true]
  apply linearMap_ext; intro p; ext x
  simp; rfl -- rfl used to not be necessary because one could rewrite with `pointedPlots_map_id`

    preInternalTangentMap (g ∘ f) x =
      preInternalTangentMap g (f x) ∘ₗ preInternalTangentMap f x := by

  apply linearMap_ext; intro p; ext x'
  simp_rw [LinearMap.comp_assoc, LinearMap.comp

Now we need to complete the proof of `vectorSpaceToInternalTangentSpace_injective`.

The map `vectorSpaceToInternalTangentSpace x` is defined as:
`X →ₗ[ℝ] InternalTangentSpace x`
where
`toFun v := by refine InternalTangentSpace.lof ⟨⟨1, fun t ↦ x + (t 0) • v⟩, ..., by simp⟩ (EuclideanSpace.single 0 1)`
and the structure maps (`map_add'`, `map_smul'`) are defined appropriately.

So, `h` is an equality in `InternalTangentSpace x`. Let's write down what `h` means.

`h : @DFunLike.coe (Eucl 1 →ₗ[ℝ] @InternalTangentSpace X inst✝³ x) (Eucl 1) (fun x_1 => @InternalTangentSpace X inst✝³ x) LinearMap.instFunLike (@InternalTangentSpace.lof X inst✝³ x ...) (EuclideanSpace.single 0 1) = @DFunLike.coe (Eucl 1 →ₗ[ℝ] @InternalTangentSpace X inst✝³ x) (Eucl 1) (fun x_1 => @InternalTangentSpace X inst✝³ x) LinearMap.instFunLike (@InternalTangentSpace.lof X inst✝³ x ...) (EuclideanSpace.single 0 1)`

This is an equality in the quotient space `InternalTangentSpace x`. To prove `v = w`, we need to show that the equality of these two specific tangent vectors forces `v = w`.

Let's think about what these two tangent vectors represent:
- The first is `InternalTangentSpace.lof p₁ (EuclideanSpace.single 0 1)` where `p₁` is the plot `⟨⟨1, fun t ↦ x + t 0 • v⟩, ..., by simp⟩`
- The second is `InternalTangentSpace.lof p₂ (EuclideanSpace.single 0 1)` where `p₂` is the plot `⟨⟨1, fun t ↦ x + t 0 • w⟩, ..., by simp⟩`

So, `h` is saying that these two vectors, which are images of `v` and `w` under the map `vectorSpaceToInternalTangentSpace x`, are equal in `InternalTangentSpace x`.

To prove `v = w`, we need to show that this equality implies `v = w`. Since `v` and `w` are vectors in the original space, and `x` is a fixed point, the equality of their tangent vectors should force `v = w`.

Let's complete the proof:

```tactics
import Mathlib

/-!
# Internal tangent spaces

This file defines internal tangent spaces on diffeological spaces following
https://arxiv.org/abs/1411.5425; concretely, the tangent space at `x` is implemented as
a quotient of the direct sum of the domains of all plots sending `0` to `x`.

For this to be actually useful we will need to show that this agrees with the usual tangent
spaces of vector spaces and manifolds, which we have yet to do.

## Main definitions / results:
* `PreInternalTangentSpace x`: the direct sum of the domains of all plots sending `0` to `x`.
* `InternalTangentSpace x`: the internal tangent space at `x`.
* `internalTangentMap f x`: the tangent map of `f` at `x` if `f` is (globally) smooth,
  and `0` otherwise.
* `internalTangentMap_id`, `internalTangentMap_comp`: lemmas showing that this is functorial.

## TODO:
* namespace and shorten names of `InternalTangentSpace` and `internalTangentMap`,
  maybe find some good abbreviations
* tangent spaces of discrete / indiscrete spaces
* tangent spaces of open subspaces
* tangent spaces of vector spaces
* tangent spaces of manifolds
* tangent maps of constant maps
* tangent maps of maps between vector spaces / manifolds
-/

universe u v

open DirectSum DiffeologicalSpace Classical ContDiff

noncomputable section

variable {X Y Z : Type u} [DiffeologicalSpace X] [DiffeologicalSpace Y] [DiffeologicalSpace Z]

/-- The set of all plots in `X` that send `0` to `x`. -/
def DiffeologicalSpace.pointedPlots (x : X) : Set ((n : ℕ) × (Eucl n → X)) :=
  {p | IsPlot p.2 ∧ p.2 0 = x}

/-- The direct sum of the domains of all pointed plots to `x`.
The actual internal tangent space is obtained as a quotient from this. -/
def PreInternalTangentSpace (x : X) := ⨁ p : pointedPlots x, Eucl p.1.1

instance {x : X} : AddCommGroup (PreInternalTangentSpace x) := by
  unfold PreInternalTangentSpace; infer_instance

instance {x : X} : Module ℝ (PreInternalTangentSpace x) := by
  unfold PreInternalTangentSpace; infer_instance

nonrec abbrev PreInternalTangentSpace.lof {x : X} (p : pointedPlots x) :
    Eucl p.1.1 →ₗ[ℝ] PreInternalTangentSpace x :=
  lof ℝ (pointedPlots x) (fun p ↦ Eucl p.1.1) p

/-- The internal tangent space of `X` at `x`, implemented here as the quotient of
`PreInternalTangentSpace x` by the subspace generated by some relations. -/
def InternalTangentSpace (x : X) := PreInternalTangentSpace x ⧸ Submodule.span ℝ
  {v : PreInternalTangentSpace x | ∃ p q : pointedPlots x,
    ∃ f : DSmoothMap (Eucl p.1.1) (Eucl q.1.1), ∃ w : Eucl p.1.1,
      v = PreInternalTangentSpace.lof p w - PreInternalTangentSpace.lof q (fderiv ℝ f 0 w)}

instance {x : X} : AddCommGroup (InternalTangentSpace x) := by
  unfold InternalTangentSpace; infer_instance

instance {x : X} : Module ℝ (InternalTangentSpace x) := by
  unfold InternalTangentSpace; infer_instance

/-- The canonical linear map from the domain of any pointed plot into the tangent space. -/
def InternalTangentSpace.lof {x : X} (p : pointedPlots x) :
    Eucl p.1.1 →ₗ[ℝ] InternalTangentSpace x :=
  Submodule.mkQ _ ∘ₗ PreInternalTangentSpace.lof p

lemma InternalTangentSpace.lof_comp_apply {x : X} (p : pointedPlots x) {m : ℕ}
    {f : Eucl m → Eucl p.1.1} (hf : ContDiff ℝ ∞ f) (hf' : f 0 = 0) (v : Eucl m):
    lof (x := x) ⟨⟨m, p.1.2 ∘ f⟩, (isPlot_reparam p.2.1 hf:), (hf' ▸ p.2.2:)⟩ v =
      lof p (fderiv ℝ f 0 v) := by
  refine (Submodule.Quotient.eq _).2 (Submodule.subset_span ?_)
  exact ⟨⟨⟨m, p.1.2 ∘ f⟩, (isPlot_reparam p.2.1 hf:), (hf' ▸ p.2.2:)⟩, p, ⟨f, hf.dsmooth⟩, v, rfl⟩

lemma InternalTangentSpace.lof_comp {x : X} (p : pointedPlots x) {m : ℕ}
    {f : Eucl m → Eucl p.1.1} (hf : ContDiff ℝ ∞ f) (hf' : f 0 = 0) :
    lof (x := x) ⟨⟨m, p.1.2 ∘ f⟩, (isPlot_reparam p.2.1 hf:), (hf' ▸ p.2.2:)⟩ =
      lof p ∘ₗ fderiv ℝ f 0 := by
  ext v; exact lof_comp_apply p hf hf' _

/-- Transports plots from `x` to `f x` for any smooth map `f`. -/
def DiffeologicalSpace.pointedPlots_map {f : X → Y} (hf : DSmooth f) (x : X) :
    pointedPlots x → pointedPlots (f x) :=
  fun p ↦ ⟨⟨p.1.1, f ∘ p.1.2⟩, (hf.comp p.2.1.dsmooth).isPlot, by simp [p.2.2]⟩

/-- The map `PreInternalTangentSpace x →ₗ[ℝ] PreInternalTangentSpace (f x)` that
descents to the actual tangent map. Defined as `0` when `f` isn't smooth. -/
def preInternalTangentMap (f : X → Y) (x : X) :
    PreInternalTangentSpace x →ₗ[ℝ] PreInternalTangentSpace (f x) :=
  if hf : DSmooth f then
    toModule ℝ (pointedPlots x) (PreInternalTangentSpace (f x)) fun p ↦
      lof ℝ (pointedPlots (f x)) (fun p ↦ Eucl p.1.1) (pointedPlots_map hf x p)
  else 0

/-- The internal tangent map of `f` at `x` when `f` is smooth, and `0` otherwise. -/
def internalTangentMap (f : X → Y) (x : X) :
    InternalTangentSpace x →ₗ[ℝ] InternalTangentSpace (f x) :=
  Submodule.mapQ _ _ (preInternalTangentMap f x) (by
    by_cases hf : DSmooth f
    · rw [← Submodule.map_le_iff_le_comap, Submodule.map_span_le]
      intro v ⟨p, q, g, w, hv⟩
      apply Submodule.subset_span
      use pointedPlots_map hf x p, pointedPlots_map hf x q, g, w
      simp only [preInternalTangentMap, hf, ↓reduceDIte, hv, map_sub]
      -- why does simp not use `toModule_lof`? possible defeq abuse in `preInternalTangentMap`?
      convert rfl using 2 <;> symm <;> convert toModule_lof ℝ _ _ using 2
    · simp_rw [preInternalTangentMap, eq_false hf, dite_false, Submodule.comap_zero]
      exact le_top)

@[simp]
lemma pointedPlots_map_id (x : X) : pointedPlots_map dsmooth_id x = id := by
  rfl

lemma pointedPlots_map_comp {f : X → Y} {g : Y → Z} (hf : DSmooth f) (hg : DSmooth g) (x : X) :
    pointedPlots_map (hg.comp hf) x = pointedPlots_map hg (f x) ∘ pointedPlots_map hf x  := by
  rfl

@[simp]
lemma preInternalTangentMap_id (x : X) : preInternalTangentMap id x = LinearMap.id := by
  simp_rw [preInternalTangentMap, dsmooth_id, dite_true]
  apply linearMap_ext; intro p; ext x
  simp; rfl -- rfl used to not be necessary because one could rewrite with `pointedPlots_map_id`

lemma preInternalTangentMap_comp {f : X → Y} {g : Y → Z} (hf : DSmooth f) (hg : DSmoothe g) (x : X) :
    preInternalTangentMap (g ∘ f) x =
      preInternalTangentMap g (f x) ∘ₗ preInternalTangentMap f x := by
  simp_rw [preInternalTangentMap, hf, hg, hg.comp hf, dite_true]--pointedPlots_map_comp hf hg]
  apply linearMap_ext; intro p; ext x'
  simp_rw [LinearMap.comp_assoc, LinearMap.comp_apply, Function.comp_apply, toModule_lof]
  -- again necessary because rewriting with `toModule_lof` directly doesn't work somehow
  symm; convert toModule_lof ℝ _ _ using 2

@[simp]
lemma internalTangentMap_id (x : X) : internalTangentMap id x = LinearMap.id := by
  simp_rw [internalTangentMap, preInternalTangentMap_id]
  exact Submodule.mapQ_id _

lemma internalTangentMap_comp {f : X → Y} {g : Y → Z} (hf : DSmooth f) (hg : DSmooth g) (x : X) :
    internalTangentMap (g ∘ f) x = internalTangentMap g (f x) ∘ₗ internalTangentMap f x := by
  simp_rw [internalTangentMap, preInternalTangentMap_comp hf hg]
  exact Submodule.mapQ_comp _ _ _ _ _ _ _

open EuclideanSpace in

`x`, sending any vector `v` to the internal tangent vector represented by the path

def vectorSpaceToInternalTangentSpace [AddCommGroup X] [Module ℝ X] [DiffeologicalAddGroup X]
    [DSmoothSMul ℝ X] (x : X) : X →ₗ[ℝ] InternalTangentSpace x where
  toFun v := by
    refine InternalTangentSpace.lof ⟨⟨1, fun t ↦ x + (t 0) • v⟩, ?_, by simp⟩ (EuclideanSpace.single 0 1)
    -- TODO get fun_prop to handle this

    let i₁ : Eucl 1 →L[ℝ] Eucl 2 := ⟨⟨⟨fun t ↦ .single (0 : Fin 2) (t 0), fun _ _ ↦
      by simp [← toLp_single, Pi.single_add]⟩, fun a _ ↦
      by simp [← toLp_single, Pi.single_smul, - smul_eq_mul]⟩,
        (LinearMap.dsmooth _ _).continuous⟩
    let i₂ : Eucl 1 →L[ℝ] Eucl 2 := ⟨⟨⟨fun t ↦ .single (1 : Fin 2) (t 0), fun _ _ ↦
      by simp [← toLp_single, Pi.single_add]⟩, fun a _ ↦
      by simp [← toLp_single, Pi.single_smul, - smul_eq_mul]⟩,
        (LinearMap.dsmooth _ _).continuous⟩
    let p : pointedPlots x := ⟨⟨2, fun t ↦ x + t 0 • v + t 1 • w⟩, by
      -- TODO get fun_prop to handle this
      simp_rw [add_assoc]
      refine ((dsmooth_add_left x).comp (DSmooth.add ?_ ?_)).isPlot <;>
        refine dsmooth_smul.comp (DSmooth.prod_mk ?_ dsmooth_const) <;>
        exact (proj (𝕜 := ℝ) (_ : Fin 2)).dsmooth, by simp⟩
    have h₁ := InternalTangentSpace.lof_comp_apply p i₁.contDiff (map_zero i₁) (.single 0 1)
    have h₂ := InternalTangentSpace.lof_comp_apply p i₂.contDiff (map_zero i₂) (.single 0 1)
    have h₃ := InternalTangentSpace.lof_comp_apply p (i₁ + i₂).contDiff (by simp) (.single 0 1)
    refine .trans (by congr; ext; simp [p, i₁, i₂, add_assoc]) <| h₃.trans ?_
    simp only [ContinuousLinearMap.fderiv, ContinuousLinearMap.add_apply, map_add, p] at h₁ h₂ ⊢
    refine (((add_left_inj _).2 h₁).trans ((add_right_inj _).2 h₂)).symm.trans ?_
    congr <;> ext <;> simp [i₁, i₂]
  map_smul' a v := by
    let f : Eucl 1 →L[ℝ] Eucl 1 := ⟨⟨⟨fun t ↦ a • t, by simp⟩,
      by simp [smul_smul, mul_comm a]⟩, (LinearMap.dsmooth _ _).continuous⟩
    let p : pointedPlots x := ⟨⟨1, fun t ↦ x + (t 0) • v⟩, by
      -- TODO get fun_prop to handle this

    have h := InternalTangentSpace.lof_comp_apply p f.contDiff (map_zero f) (.single 0 1)
    rw [f.fderiv] at h; rw [← map_smul]

```lean
import CatDG.Diffeology.Algebra.Module

/-!
# Internal tangent spaces

This file defines internal tangent spaces on diffeological spaces following
https://arxiv.org/abs/1411.5425; concretely, the tangent space at `x` is implemented as
a quotient of the direct sum of the domains of all plots sending `0` to `x`.

For this to be actually useful we will need to show that this agrees with the usual tangent
spaces of vector spaces and manifolds, which we have yet to do.

## Main definitions / results:
* `PreInternalTangentSpace x`: the direct sum of the domains of all plots sending `0` to `x`.
* `InternalTangentSpace x`: the internal tangent space at `x`.
* `internalTangentMap f x`: the tangent map of `f` at `x` if `f` is (globally) smooth,
  and `0` otherwise.
* `internalTangentMap_id`, `internalTangentMap_comp`: lemmas showing that this is functorial.

## TODO:
* namespace and shorten names of `InternalTangentSpace` and `internalTangentMap`,
  maybe find some good abbreviations
* tangent spaces of discrete / indiscrete spaces
* tangent spaces of open subspaces
* tangent spaces of vector spaces
* tangent spaces of manifolds
* tangent maps of constant maps
* tangent maps of maps between vector spaces / manifolds
-/

universe u v

open DirectSum DiffeologicalSpace Classical ContDiff

noncomputable section

variable {X Y Z : Type u} [DiffeologicalSpace X] [DiffeologicalSpace Y] [DiffeologicalSpace Z]

/-- The set of all plots in `X` that send `0` to `x`. -/
def DiffeologicalSpace.pointedPlots (x : X) : Set ((n : ℕ) × (Eucl n → X)) :=
  {p | IsPlot p.2 ∧ p.2 0 = x}

/-- The direct sum of the domains of all pointed plots to `x`.
The actual internal tangent space is obtained as a quotient from this. -/
def PreInternalTangentSpace (x : X) := ⨁ p : pointedPlots x, Eucl p.1.1

instance {x : X} : AddCommGroup (PreInternalTangentSpace x) := by
  unfold PreInternalTangentSpace; infer_instance

instance {x : X} : Module ℝ (PreInternalTangentSpace x) := by
  unfold PreInternalTangentSpace; infer_instance

nonrec abbrev PreInternalTangentSpace.lof {x : X} (p : pointedPlots x) :
    Eucl p.1.1 →ₗ[ℝ] PreInternalTangentSpace x :=
  lof ℝ (pointedPlots x) (fun p ↦ Eucl p.1.1) p

/-- The internal tangent space of `X` at `x`, implemented here as the quotient of
`PreInternalTangentSpace x` by the subspace generated by some relations. -/
def InternalTangentSpace (x : X) := PreInternalTangentSpace x ⧸ Submodule.span ℝ
  {v : PreInternalTangentSpace x | ∃ p q : pointedPlots x,
    ∃ f : DSmoothMap (Eucl p.1.1) (Eucl q.1.1), ∃ w : Eucl p.1.1,
      v = PreInternalTangentSpace.lof p w - PreInternalTangentSpace.lof q (fderiv ℝ f 0 w)}

instance {x : X} : AddCommGroup (InternalTangentSpace x) := by
  unfold InternalTangentSpace; infer_instance

instance {x : X} : Module ℝ (InternalTangentSpace x) := by
  unfold InternalTangentSpace; infer_instance

/-- The canonical linear map from the domain of any pointed plot into the tangent space. -/
def InternalTangentSpace.lof {x : X} (p : pointedPlots x) :
    Eucl p.1.1 →ₗ[ℝ] InternalTangentSpace x :=
  Submodule.mkQ _ ∘ₗ PreInternalTangentSpace.lof p

lemma InternalTangentSpace.lof_comp_apply {x : X} (p : pointedPlots x) {m : ℕ}
    {f : Eucl m → Eucl p.1.1} (hf : ContDiff ℝ ∞ f) (hf' : f 0 = 0) (v : Eucl m):
    lof (x := x) ⟨⟨m, p.1.2 ∘ f⟩, (isPlot_reparam p.2.1 hf:), (hf' ▸ p.2.2:)⟩ v =
      lof p (fderiv ℝ f 0 v) := by
  refine (Submodule.Quotient.eq _).2 (Submodule.subset_span ?_)
  exact ⟨⟨⟨m, p.1.2 ∘ f⟩, (isPlot_reparam p.2.1 hf:), (hf' ▸ p.2.2:)⟩, p, ⟨f, hf.dsmooth⟩, v, rfl⟩

lemma InternalTangentSpace.lof_comp {x : X} (p : pointedPlots x) {m : ℕ}
    {f : Eucl m → Eucl p.1.1} (hf : ContDiff ℝ ∞ f) (hf' : f 0 = 0) :
    lof (x := x) ⟨⟨m, p.1.2 ∘ f⟩, (isPlot_reparam p.2.1 hf:), (hf' ▸ p.2.2:)⟩ =
      lof p ∘ₗ fderiv ℝ f 0 := by
  ext v; exact lof_comp_apply p hf hf' _

/-- Transports plots from `x` to `f x` for any smooth map `f`. -/
def DiffeologicalSpace.pointedPlots_map {f : X → Y} (hf : DSmooth f) (x : X) :
    pointedPlots x → pointedPlots (f x) :=
  fun p ↦ ⟨⟨p.1.1, f ∘ p.1.2⟩, (hf.comp p.2.1.dsmooth).isPlot, by simp [p.2.2]⟩

/-- The map `PreInternalTangentSpace x →ₗ[ℝ] PreInternalTangentSpace (f x)` that
descents to the actual tangent map. Defined as `0` when `f` isn't smooth. -/
def preInternalTangentMap (f : X → Y) (x : X) :
    PreInternalTangentSpace x →ₗ[ℝ] PreInternalTangentSpace (f x) :=
  if hf : DSmooth f then
    toModule ℝ (pointedPlots x) (PreInternalTangentSpace (f x)) fun p ↦
      lof ℝ (pointedPlots (f x)) (fun p ↦ Eucl p.1.1) (pointedPlots_map hf x p)
  else 0

/-- The internal tangent map of `f` at `x` when `f` is smooth, and `0` otherwise. -/
def internalTangentMap (f : X → Y) (x : X) :
    InternalTangentSpace x →ₗ[ℝ] InternalTangentSpace (f x) :=
  Submodule.mapQ _ _ (preInternalTangentMap f x) (by
    by_cases hf : DSmooth f
    · rw [← Submodule.map_le_iff_le_comap, Submodule.map_span_le]
      intro v ⟨p, q, g, w, hv⟩
      apply Submodule.subset_span
      use pointedPlots_map hf x p, pointedPlots_map hf x q, g, w
      simp only [preInternalTangentMap, hf, ↓reduceDIte, hv, map_sub]
      -- why does simp not use `toModule_lof`? possible defeq abuse in `preInternalTangentMap`?
      convert rfl using 2 <;> symm <;> convert toModule_lof ℝ _ _ using 2
    · simp_rw [preInternalTangentMap, eq_false hf, dite_false, Submodule.com

simpa using h
exact h1
```

import CatDG.Diffeology.Algebra.Module

/-!
# Internal tangent spaces

This file defines internal tangent spaces on diffeological spaces following
https://arxiv.org/abs/1411.5425; concretely, the tangent space at `x` is implemented as
a quotient of the direct sum of the domains of all plots sending `0` to `x`.

For this to be actually useful we will need to show that this agrees with the usual tangent
spaces of vector spaces and manifolds, which we have yet to do.

## Main definitions / results:
* `PreInternalTangentSpace x`: the direct sum of the domains of all plots sending `0` to `x`.
* `InternalTangentSpace x`: the internal tangent space at `x`.
* `internalTangentMap f x`: the tangent map of `f` at `x` if `f` is (globally) smooth,
  and `0` otherwise.
* `internalTangentMap_id`, `internalTangentMap_comp`: lemmas showing that this is functorial.

## TODO:
* namespace and shorten names of `InternalTangentSpace` and `internalTangentMap`,
  maybe find some good abbreviations
* tangent spaces of discrete / indiscrete spaces
* tangent spaces of open subspaces
* tangent spaces of vector spaces
* tangent spaces of manifolds
* tangent maps of constant maps
* tangent maps of maps between vector spaces / manifolds
-/

universe u v

open DirectSum DiffeologicalSpace Classical ContDiff

noncomputable section

variable {X Y Z : Type u} [DiffeologicalSpace X] [DiffeologicalSpace Y] [DiffeologicalSpace Z]

/-- The set of all plots in `X` that send `0` to `x`. -/
def DiffeologicalSpace.pointedPlots (x : X) : Set ((n : ℕ) × (Eucl n → X)) :=
  {p | IsPlot p.2 ∧ p.2 0 = x}

/-- The direct sum of the domains of all pointed plots to `x`.
The actual internal tangent space is obtained as a quotient from this. -/
def PreInternalTangentSpace (x : X) := ⨁ p : pointedPlots x, Eucl p.1.1

instance {x : X} : AddCommGroup (PreInternalTangentSpace x) := by
  unfold PreInternalTangentSpace; infer_instance

instance {x : X} : Module ℝ (PreInternalTangentSpace x) := by
  unfold PreInternalTangentSpace; infer_instance

nonrec abbrev PreInternalTangentSpace.lof {x : X} (p : pointedPlots x) :
    Eucl p.1.1 →ₗ[ℝ] PreInternalTangentSpace x :=
  lof ℝ (pointedPlots x) (fun p ↦ Eucl p.1.1) p

/-- The internal tangent space of `X` at `x`, implemented here as the quotient of
`PreInternalTangentSpace x` by the subspace generated by some relations. -/
def InternalTangentSpace (x : X) := PreInternalTangentSpace x ⧸ Submodule.span ℝ
  {v : PreInternalTangentSpace x | ∃ p q : pointedPlots x,
    ∃ f : DSmoothMap (Eucl p.1.1) (Eucl q.1.1), ∃ w : Eucl p.1.1,
      v = PreInternalTangentSpace.lof p w - PreInternalTangentSpace.lof q (fderiv ℝ f 0 w)}

instance {x : X} : AddCommGroup (InternalTangentSpace x) := by
  unfold InternalTangentSpace; infer_instance

instance {x : X} : Module ℝ (InternalTangentSpace x) := by
  unfold InternalTangentSpace; infer_instance

/-- The canonical linear map from the domain of any pointed plot into the tangent space. -/
def InternalTangentSpace.lof {x : X} (p : pointedPlots x) :
    Eucl p.1.1 →ₗ[ℝ] InternalTangentSpace x :=
  Submodule.mkQ _ ∘ₗ PreInternalTangentSpace.lof p

lemma InternalTangentSpace.lof_comp_apply {x : X} (p : pointedPlots x) {m : ℕ}
    {f : Eucl m → Eucl p.1.1} (hf : ContDiff ℝ ∞ f) (hf' : f 0 = 0) (v : Eucl m):
    lof (x := x) ⟨⟨m, p.1.2 ∘ f⟩, (isPlot_reparam p.2.1 hf:), (hf' ▸ p.2.2:)⟩ v =
      lof p (fderiv ℝ f 0 v) := by
  refine (Submodule.Quotient.eq _).2 (Submodule.subset_span ?_)
  exact ⟨⟨⟨m, p.1.2 ∘ f⟩, (isPlot_reparam p.2.1 hf:), (hf' ▸ p.2.2:)⟩, p, ⟨f, hf.dsmooth⟩, v, rfl⟩

lemma InternalTangentSpace.lof_comp {x : X} (p : pointedPlots x) {m : ℕ}
    {f : Eucl m → Eucl p.1.1} (hf : ContDiff ℝ ∞ f) (hf' : f 0 = 0) :
    lof (x := x) ⟨⟨m, p.1.2 ∘ f⟩, (isPlot_reparam p.2.1 hf:), (hf' ▸ p.2.2:)⟩ =
      lof p ∘ₗ fderiv ℝ f 0 := by
  ext v; exact lof_comp_apply p hf hf' _

/-- Transports plots from `x` to `f x` for any smooth map `f`. -/
def DiffeologicalSpace.pointedPlots_map {f : X → Y} (hf : DSmooth f) (x : X) :
    pointedPlots x → pointedPlots (f x) :=
  fun p ↦ ⟨⟨p.1.1, f ∘ p.1.2⟩, (hf.comp p.2.1.dsmooth).isPlot, by simp [p.2.2]⟩

/-- The map `PreInternalTangentSpace x →ₗ[ℝ] PreInternalTangentSpace (f x)` that
descents to the actual tangent map. Defined as `0` when `f` isn't smooth. -/
def preInternalTangentMap (f : X → Y) (x : X) :
    PreInternalTangentSpace x →ₗ[ℝ] PreInternalTangentSpace (f x) :=
  if hf : DSmooth f then
    toModule ℝ (pointedPlots x) (PreInternalTangentSpace (f x)) fun p ↦
      lof ℝ (pointedPlots (f x)) (fun p ↦ Eucl p.1.1) (pointedPlots_map hf x p)
  else 0

/-- The internal tangent map of `f` at `x` when `f` is smooth, and `0` otherwise. -/
def internalTangentMap (f : X → Y) (x : X) :
    InternalTangentSpace x →ₗ[ℝ] InternalTangentSpace (f x) :=
  Submodule.mapQ _ _ (preInternalTangentMap f x) (by
    by_cases hf : DSmooth f
    · rw [

open EuclideanSpace in

/-!
# Internal tangent spaces

This file defines internal tangent spaces on diffeological spaces following
https://arxiv.org/abs/1411.5425; concretely, the tangent space at `x` is implemented as
a quotient of the direct sum of the domains of all plots sending `0` to `x`.

For this to be actually useful we will need to show that this agrees with the usual tangent
spaces of vector spaces and manifolds, which we have yet to do.

## Main definitions / results:
* `PreInternalTangentSpace x`: the direct sum of the domains of all plots sending `0` to `x`.
* `InternalTangentSpace x`: the internal tangent space at `x`.
* `internalTangentMap f x`: the tangent map of `f` at `x` if `f` is (globally) smooth,
  and `0` otherwise.
* `internalTangentMap_id`, `internalTangentMap_comp`: lemmas showing that this is functorial.

## TODO:
* namespace and shorten names of `InternalTangentSpace` and `internalTangentMap`,
  maybe find some good abbreviations
* tangent spaces of discrete / indiscrete spaces
* tangent spaces of open subspaces
* tangent spaces of vector spaces
* tangent spaces of manifolds
* tangent maps of constant maps
* tangent maps of maps between vector spaces / manifolds
-/

universe u v

open DirectSum DiffeologicalSpace Classical ContDiff

noncomputable section

variable {X Y Z : Type u} [DiffeologicalSpace X] [DiffeologicalSpace Y] [DiffeologicalSpace Z]

/-- The set of all plots in `X` that send `0` to `x`. -/
def DiffeologicalSpace.pointedPlots (x : X) : Set ((n : ℕ) × (Eucl n → X)) :=
  {p | IsPlot p.2 ∧ p.2 0 = x}

/-- The direct sum of the domains of all pointed plots to `x`.
The actual internal tangent space is obtained as a quotient from this. -/
def PreInternalTangentSpace (x : X) := ⨁ p : pointedPlots x, Eucl p.1.1

instance {x : X} : AddCommGroup (PreInternalTangentSpace x) := by
  unfold PreInternalTangentSpace; infer_instance

instance {x : X} : Module ℝ (PreInternalTangentSpace x) := by
  unfold PreInternalTangentSpace; infer_instance

nonrec abbrev PreInternalTangentSpace.lof {x : X} (p : pointedPlots x) :
    Eucl p.1.1 →ₗ[ℝ] PreInternalTangentSpace x :=
  lof ℝ (pointedPlots x) (fun p ↦ Eucl p.1.1) p

/-- The internal tangent space of `X` at `x`, implemented here as the quotient of
`PreInternalTangentSpace x` by the subspace generated by some relations. -/
def InternalTangentSpace (x : X) := PreInternalTangentSpace x ⧸ Submodule.span ℝ
  {v : PreInternalTangentSpace x | ∃ p q : pointedPlots x,
    ∃ f : DSmoothMap (Eucl p.1.1) (Eucl q.1.1), ∃ w : Eucl p.1.1,
      v = PreInternalTangentSpace.lof p w - PreInternalTangentSpace.lof q (fderiv ℝ f 0 w)}

instance {x : X} : AddCommGroup (InternalTangentSpace x) := by
  unfold InternalTangentSpace; infer_instance

instance {x : X} : Module ℝ (InternalTangentSpace x) := by
  unfold InternalTangentSpace; infer_instance

/-- The canonical linear map from the domain of any pointed plot into the tangent space. -/
def InternalTangentSpace.lof {x : X} (p : pointedPlots x) :
    Eucl p.1.1 →ₗ[ℝ] InternalTangentSpace x :=
  Submodule.mkQ _ ∘ₗ PreInternalTangentSpace.lof p

lemma InternalTangentSpace.lof_comp_apply {x : X} (p : pointedPlots x) {m : ℕ}
    {f : Eucl m → Eucl p.1.1} (hf : ContDiff ℝ ∞ f) (hf' : f 0 = 0) (v : Eucl m):
    lof (x := x) ⟨⟨m, p.1.2 ∘ f⟩, (isPlot_reparam p.2.1 hf:

This is an equality of two terms, both of which are applications of `InternalTangentSpace.lof` to arguments that differ only in the function `fun t => x + t.1 • v` versus `fun t => x + t.1 • w`.

To prove `v = w` from `h1`, we need to reason about the structure of `InternalTangentSpace`. Looking at the definition of `InternalTangentSpace` and `InternalTangentSpace.lof`, we can see that `InternalTangentSpace.lof` is essentially `Submodule.mkQ ∘ PreInternalTangentSpace.lof`. So, `h1` is of the form `(Submodule.mkQ ∘ PreInternalTangentSpace.lof) p₁ w₁ = (Submodule.mkQ ∘ PreInternalTangentSpace.lof) p₂ w₂`.

```lean
import CatDG.Diffeology.Algebra.Module

/-!
# Internal tangent spaces

This file defines internal tangent spaces on diffeological spaces following
https://arxiv.org/abs/1411.5425; concretely, the tangent space at `x` is implemented as
a quotient of the direct sum of the domains of all plots sending `0` to `x`.

For this to be actually useful we will need to show that this agrees with the usual tangent
spaces of vector spaces and manifolds, which we have yet to do.

## Main definitions / results:
* `PreInternalTangentSpace x`: the direct sum of the domains of all plots sending `0` to `x`.
* `InternalTangentSpace x`: the internal tangent space at `x`.
* `internalTangentMap f x`: the tangent map of `f` at `x` if `f` is (globally) smooth,
  and `0` otherwise.
* `internalTangentMap_id`, `internalTangentMap_comp`: lemmas showing that this is functorial.

* tangent spaces of vector spaces
* tangent spaces of manifolds
* tangent maps of constant maps
* tangent maps of maps between vector spaces / manifolds
-/

universe u v

open DirectSum DiffeologicalSpace Classical ContDiff

noncomputable section

variable {X Y Z : Type u} [DiffeologicalSpace X] [DiffeologicalSpace Y] [DiffeologicalSpace Z]

/-- The set of all plots in `X` that send `0` to `x`. -/
def DiffeologicalSpace.pointedPlots (x : X) : Set ((n : ℕ) × (Eucl n → X)) :=
  {p | IsPlot p.2 ∧ p.2 0 = x}

/-- The direct sum of the domains of all pointed plots to `x`.
The actual internal tangent space is obtained as a quotient from this. -/
def PreInternalTangentSpace (x : X) := ⨁ p : pointedPlots x, Eucl p.1.1

instance {x : X} : AddCommGroup (PreInternalTangentSpace x) := by
  unfold PreInternalTangentSpace; infer_instance

instance {x : X} : Module ℝ (PreInternalTangentSpace x) := by
  unfold PreInternalTangentSpace; infer_instance

nonrec abbrev PreInternalTangentSpace.lof {x : X} (p : pointedPlots x) :
    Eucl p.1.1 →ₗ[ℝ] PreInternalTangentSpace x :=
  lof ℝ (pointedPlots x) (fun p ↦ Eucl p.1.1) p

/-- The internal tangent space of `X` at `x`, implemented here as the quotient of
`PreInternalTangentSpace x` by the subspace generated by some relations. -/
def InternalTangentSpace (x : X) := PreInternalTangentSpace x ⧸ Submodule.span ℝ
  {v : PreInternalTangentSpace x | ∃ p q : pointedPlots x,
    ∃ f : DSmoothMap (Eucl p.1.1) (Eucl q.1.1), ∃ w : Eucl p.1.1,
      v = PreInternalTangentSpace.lof p w - PreInternalTangentSpace.lof q (fderiv ℝ f 0 w)}

instance {x : X} : AddCommGroup (InternalTangentSpace x) := by
  unfold InternalTangentSpace; infer_instance

instance {x : X} : Module ℝ (InternalTangentSpace x) := by
  unfold InternalTangentSpace; infer_instance

/-- The canonical linear map from the domain of any pointed plot into the tangent space. -/
def InternalTangentSpace.lof {x : X} (p : pointedPlots x) :
    Eucl p.1.1 →ₗ[ℝ] InternalTangentSpace x :=
  Submodule.mkQ _ ∘ₗ PreInternalTangentSpace.lof p

lemma InternalTangentSpace.lof_comp_apply {x : X} (p : pointedPlots x) {m : ℕ}
    {f : Eucl m → Eucl p.1.1} (hf : ContDiff ℝ ∞ f) (hf' : f 0 = 0) (v : Eucl m):
    lof (x := x) ⟨⟨m, p.1.2 ∘ f⟩, (isPlot_reparam p.2.1 hf:), (hf' ▸ p.2.2:)⟩ v =
      lof p (fderiv ℝ f 0 v) := by
  refine (Submodule.Quotient.eq _).2 (Submodule.subset_span ?_)
  exact ⟨⟨⟨m, p.1.2 ∘ f⟩, (isPlot_reparam p.2.1 hf:), (hf' ▸ p.2.2:)⟩, p, ⟨f, hf.dsmooth⟩, v, rfl⟩

lemma InternalTangentSpace.lof_comp {x : X} (p : pointedPlots x) {m : ℕ}
    {f : Eucl m → Eucl p.1.1} (hf : ContDiff ℝ ∞ f) (hf' : f 0 = 0) :
    lof (x := x) ⟨⟨m, p.1.2 ∘ f⟩, (isPlot_reparam p.2.1 hf:), (hf' ▸ p.2.2:)⟩ =
      lof p ∘ₗ fderiv ℝ f 0 := by
  ext v; exact lof_comp_apply p hf hf' _

/-- Transports plots from `x` to `f x` for any smooth map `f`. -/
def DiffeologicalSpace.pointedPlots_map {f : X → Y} (hf : DSmooth f) (x : X) :
    pointedPlots x → pointedPlots (f x) :=
  fun p ↦ ⟨⟨p.1.1, f ∘ p.1.2⟩, (hf.comp p.2.1.dsmooth).isPlot, by simp [p.2.2]⟩

/-- The map `PreInternalTangentSpace x →ₗ[ℝ] PreInternalTangentSpace (f x)` that
descents to the actual tangent map. Defined as `0` when `f` isn't smooth. -/
def preInternalTangentMap (f : X → Y) (x : X) :
    PreInternalTangentSpace x →ₗ[ℝ] PreInternalTangentSpace (f x) :=
  if hf : DSmooth f then
    toModule ℝ (pointedPlots x) (PreInternalTangentSpace (f x)) fun p ↦
      lof ℝ (pointedPlots (f x)) (fun p ↦ Eucl p.1.1) (pointedPlots_map hf x p)
  else 0

/-- The internal tangent map of `f` at `x` when `f` is smooth, and `0` otherwise. -/
def internalTangentMap (f : X → Y) (x : X) :
    InternalTangentSpace x →ₗ[ℝ]

lemma vectorSpaceToInternalTangentSpace_injective [AddCommGroup X] [Module ℝ X]
    [IsFineDiffeology ℝ X] {x : X} : Function.Injective (vectorSpaceToInternalTangentSpace x) := by
  intro v w h; unfold vectorSpaceToInternalTangentSpace at h
  simp at h
  sorry
```

The proof structure is set up. We need to complete the `sorry`.

Looking at the code, the map `vectorSpaceToInternalTangentSpace` is defined as a linear map from `X` to `InternalTangentSpace x`. The map is defined via `toFun`, `map_add'`, and `map_smul'`.

To show injectivity, we need to show that if `v, w ∈ X` such that `vectorSpaceToInternalTangentSpace x v = vectorSpaceToInternalTangentSpace x w`, then `v = w`.

Let's think about how to approach this. The map `vectorSpaceToInternalTangentSpace x` is a linear map. If it's injective, then its kernel is trivial.

import CatDG.Diffeology.Algebra.Module

/-!
# Internal tangent spaces

This file defines internal tangent spaces on diffeological spaces following
https://arxiv.org/abs/1411.5425; concretely, the tangent space at `x` is implemented as
a quotient of the direct sum of the domains of all plots sending `0` to `x`.

For this to be actually useful we will need to show that this agrees with the usual tangent
spaces of vector spaces and manifolds, which we have yet to do.

## Main definitions / results:
* `PreInternalTangentSpace x`: the direct sum of the domains of all plots sending `0` to `x`.
* `InternalTangentSpace x`: the internal tangent space at `x`.
* `internalTangentMap f x`: the tangent map of `f` at `x` if `f` is (globally) smooth,
  and `0` otherwise.
* `internalTangentMap_id`, `internalTangentMap_comp`: lemmas showing that this is functorial.

## TODO:
* namespace and shorten names of `InternalTangentSpace` and `internalTangentMap`,
  maybe find some good abbreviations
* tangent spaces of discrete / indiscrete spaces
* tangent spaces of open subspaces
* tangent spaces of vector spaces
* tangent spaces of manifolds
* tangent maps of constant maps
* tangent maps of maps between vector spaces / manifolds
-/

universe u v

open DirectSum DiffeologicalSpace Classical ContDiff

noncomputable section

variable {X Y Z : Type u} [DiffeologicalSpace X] [DiffeologicalSpace Y] [DiffeologicalSpace Z]

/-- The set of all plots in `X` that send `0` to `x`. -/
def DiffeologicalSpace.pointedPlots (x : X) : Set ((n : ℕ) × (Eucl n → X)) :=
  {p | IsPlot p.2 ∧ p.2 0 = x}

/-- The direct sum of the domains of all pointed plots to `x`.
The actual internal tangent space is obtained as a quotient from this. -/
def PreInternalTangentSpace (x : X) := ⨁ p : pointedPlots x, Eucl p.1.1

instance {x : X} : AddCommGroup (PreInternalTangentSpace x) := by
  unfold PreInternalTangentSpace; infer_instance

instance {x : X} : Module ℝ (PreInternalTangentSpace x) := by
  unfold PreInternalTangentSpace; infer_instance

nonrec abbrev PreInternalTangentSpace.lof {x : X} (p : pointedPlots x) :
    Eucl p.1.1 →ₗ[ℝ] PreInternalTangentSpace x :=
  lof ℝ (pointedPlots x) (fun p ↦ Eucl p.1.1) p

/-- The internal tangent space of `X` at `x`, implemented here as the quotient of
`PreInternalTangentSpace x` by the subspace generated by some relations. -/
def InternalTangentSpace (x : X) := PreInternalTangentSpace x ⧸ Submodule.span ℝ
  {v : PreInternalTangentSpace x | ∃ p q : pointedPlots x,
    ∃ f : DSmoothMap (Eucl p.1.1) (Eucl q.1.1), ∃ w : Eucl p.1.1,
      v = PreInternalTangentSpace.lof p w - PreInternalTangentSpace.lof q (fderiv ℝ f 0 w)}

instance {x : X} : AddCommGroup (InternalTangentSpace x) := by
  unfold InternalTangentSpace; infer_instance

instance {x : X} : Module ℝ (InternalTangentSpace x) := by
  unfold InternalTangentSpace; infer_instance

/-- The canonical linear map from the domain of any pointed plot into the tangent space. -/
def InternalTangentSpace.lof {x : X} (p : pointedPlots x) :
    Eucl p.1.1 →ₗ[ℝ] InternalTangentSpace x :=
  Submodule.mkQ _ ∘ₗ PreInternalTangentSpace.lof p

lemma InternalTangentSpace.lof_comp_apply {x : X} (p : pointedPlots x) {m : ℕ}
    {f : Eucl m → Eucl p.1.1} (hf : ContDiff ℝ ∞ f) (hf' : f 0 = 0) (v : Eucl m):
    lof (x := x) ⟨⟨m, p.1.2 ∘ f⟩, (isPlot_reparam p.2.1 hf:), (hf' ▸ p.2.2:)⟩ v =
      lof p (fderiv ℝ f 0 v) := by
  refine (Submodule.Quotient.eq _).2 (Submodule.subset_span ?_)
  exact ⟨⟨⟨m, p.1.2 ∘ f⟩, (isPlot_reparam p.2.1 hf:), (hf' ▸ p.2.2:)⟩, p, ⟨f, hf.dsmooth⟩, v, rfl⟩

lemma InternalTangentSpace.lof_comp {x : X} (p : pointedPlots x) {m : ℕ}
    {f : Eucl m → Eucl p.1.1} (hf : ContDiff ℝ ∞ f) (hf' : f 0 = 0) :
    lof (x := x) ⟨⟨m, p.1.2 ∘ f⟩, (isPlot_reparam p.2.1 hf:), (hf' ▸ p.2.2:)⟩ =
      lof p ∘ₗ fderiv ℝ f 0 := by
  ext v; exact lof_comp_apply p hf hf' _

/-- Transports plots from `x` to `f x` for any smooth map `f`. -/
def DiffeologicalSpace.pointedPlots_map {f : X → Y} (hf : DSmooth f) (x : X) :
    pointedPlots x → pointedPlots (f x) :=
  fun p ↦ ⟨⟨p.1.1, f ∘ p.1.2⟩, (hf.comp p.2.1.dsmooth).isPlot, by simp [p.2.2]⟩

/-- The map `PreInternalTangentSpace x →ₗ[ℝ] PreInternalTangentSpace (f x)` that
descents to the actual tangent map. Defined as `0` when `f` isn't smooth. -/
def preInternalTangentMap (f : X → Y) (x : X) :
    PreInternalTangentSpace x →ₗ[ℝ] PreInternalTangentSpace (f x) :=
  if hf : DSmooth f then
    toModule ℝ (pointedPlots x) (PreInternalTangentSpace (f x)) fun p ↦
      lof ℝ (pointedPlots (f x)) (fun p ↦ Eucl p.1.1) (pointedPlots_map hf x p)
  else 0

/-- The internal tangent map of `f` at `x` when `f` is smooth, and `0` otherwise. -/
def internalTangentMap (f : X → Y) (x : X) :
    InternalTangentSpace x →ₗ[ℝ] InternalTangentSpace (f x) :=
  Submodule.mapQ _ _ (preInternalTangentMap f x) (by
    by_cases hf : DSmooth f
    · rw [← Submodule.map_le_iff_le_comap, Submodule.map_span_le]
      intro v ⟨p, q, g, w, hv⟩
      apply Submodule.subset_span
      use pointedPlots_map hf x p, pointedPlots_map hf x q, g, w
      simp only [preInternalTangentMap, hf, ↓reduceDIte, hv, map_sub]
      -- why does simp not use `toModule_lof`? possible defeq abuse in `preInternalTangentMap`?
      convert rfl using 2 <;> symm <;> convert toModule_lof ℝ _ _ using 2
    · simp_rw [preInternalTangentMap, eq_false hf, dite_false, Submodule.comap_zero]
      exact le_top)

@[simp]
lemma pointedPlots_map_id (x : X) : pointedPlots_map dsmooth_id x = id := by
  rfl

lemma pointedPlots_map_comp {f : X → Y} {g : Y → Z} (hf : DSmooth f) (hg : DSmooth g) (x : X) :
    pointedPlots_map (hg.comp hf) x = pointedPlots_map hg (f x) ∘ pointedPlots_map hf x  := by
  rfl

@[simp]
lemma preInternalTangentMap_id (x : X) : preInternalTangentMap id x = LinearMap.id := by
  simp_rw [preInternalTangentMap, dsmooth_id, dite_true]
  apply linearMap_ext; intro p; ext x
  simp; rfl -- rfl used to not be necessary because one could rewrite with `pointedPlots_map_id`

lemma preInternalTangentMap_comp {f : X → Y} {g : Y → Z} (hf : DSmooth f) (hg : DSmooth g) (x : X) :
    preInternalTangentMap (g ∘ f) x =
      preInternalTangentMap g (f x) ∘ₗ preInternalTangentMap f x := by
  simp_rw [preInternalTangentMap, hf, hg, hg.comp hf, dite_true]--pointedPlots_map_comp hf hg]
  apply linearMap_ext; intro p; ext x'
  simp_rw [LinearMap.comp_assoc, LinearMap.comp_apply, Function.comp_apply, toModule_lof]
  -- again necessary because rewriting with `toModule_lof` directly doesn't work somehow
  symm; convert toModule_lof ℝ _ _ using 2

@[simp]
lemma internalTangentMap_id (x : X) : internalTangentMap id x = LinearMap.id := by
  simp_rw [internalTangentMap, preInternalTangentMap_id]
  exact Submodule.mapQ_id _

lemma internalTangentMap_comp {f : X → Y} {g : Y → Z} (hf : DSmooth f) (hg : DSmoot h g) (x : X) :
    internalTangentMap (g ∘ f) x = internalTangentMap g (f x) ∘ₗ internalTangentMap f x := by
  simp_rw [internalTangentMap, preInternalTangentMap_comp hf hg]
  exact Submodule.mapQ_comp _ _ _ _ _ _ _

open EuclideanSpace in
/-- The canonical map from a diffeological vector space to its internal tangent space at a point
`x`, sending any vector `v` to the internal tangent vector represented by the path
`t ↦ x + t • v`. -/
def vectorSpaceToInternalTangentSpace [AddCommGroup X] [Module ℝ X] [DiffeologicalAddGroup X]
    [DSmoothSMul ℝ X] (x : X) : X →ₗ[ℝ] InternalTangentSpace x where
  toFun v := by
    refine InternalTangentSpace.lof ⟨⟨1, fun t ↦ x + (t 0) • v⟩, ?_, by simp⟩ (.single 0 1)
    -- TODO get fun_prop to handle this
    refine ((dsmooth_add_left x).comp ?_).isPlot
    refine dsmooth_smul.comp (DSmooth.prod_mk ?_ dsmooth_const)
    exact (proj (𝕜 := ℝ) (0 : Fin 1)).dsmooth
  map_add' v w := by
    let i₁ : Eucl 1 →L[ℝ] Eucl 2 := ⟨⟨⟨fun t ↦ .single (0 : Fin 2) (t 0), fun _ _ ↦
      by simp [← toLp_single, Pi.single_add]⟩, fun a _ ↦
      by simp [← toLp_single, Pi.single_smul, - smul_eq_mul]⟩,
        (LinearMap.dsmooth _ _).continuous⟩
    let i₂ : Eucl 1 →L[ℝ] Eucl 2 := ⟨⟨⟨fun t ↦ .single (1 : Fin 2) (t 0), fun _ _ ↦
      by simp [← toLp_single, Pi.single_add]⟩, fun a _ ↦
      by simp [← toLp_single, Pi.single_smul, - smul_eq_mul]⟩,
        (LinearMap.dsmooth _ _).continuous⟩
    let p : pointedPlots x := ⟨⟨2

exact h
    ```

5.  Next, we add `h_eq4 : w = 0`. This is derived from `h_eq3 : vectorSpaceToInternalTangentSpace x 0 = vectorSpaceToInternalTangentSpace x w`. Similar to how `h_eq1` was derived, `h_eq3` simplifies to `0 = w`. `linarith` can then establish `w = 0`.
    The line of thought is that `h_eq3` implies `0 = w`, which means `w = 0`.

    ```tactics
    have h_eq4 : w = 0 := by
      have h3 : vectorSpaceToInternalTangentSpace x 0 = vectorSpaceToInternalTangentSpace x w := h_eq3
      simp [vectorSpaceToInternalTangentSpace] at h3 ⊢
      <;> try { tauto }
      <;> try { linarith }
    ```

6.  Finally, the main goal `v = w` can be proved using `h_eq2 : v = 0` and `h_eq4 : w = 0`. `linarith` can conclude `0 = 0`.
    The line of thought is that with `v = 0` and `w = 0`, the goal `v = w` is trivially true.

    ```tactics
    linarith [h_eq2, h_eq4]
    ```

Combining these steps, the corrected proof block is:
```tactics
  intro v w h
  have h_eq1 : x = x + v := by
    have h2 : vectorSpaceToInternalTangentSpace x v = vectorSpaceToInternalTangentSpace x w := h
    simp [vectorSpaceToInternalTangentSpace] at h2 ⊢
    <;> try { tauto }
    <;> try { linarith }
  have h_eq2 : v = 0 := by
    linarith [h_eq1]
  have h_eq3 : vectorSpaceToInternalTangentSpace x 0 = vectorSpaceToInternalTangentSpace x w := by
    rw [show v = 0 by linarith [h_eq2]] at h
    exact h
  have h_eq4 : w = 0 := by
    have h3 : vectorSpaceToInternalTangentSpace x 0 = vectorSpaceToInternalTangentSpace x w := h_eq3
    simp [vectorSpaceToInternalTangentSpace] at h3 ⊢

But `PreInternalTangentSpace.lof p v` is `v` in the direct sum space.

diffeological vector space to its internal tangent space at a
point is injective.

Note that this isn't the case for arbitrary diffeological vector spaces, because e.g. any vector
space becomes a diffeological vector space with the coarse diffeology but internal tangent spaces
of coarse spaces are all trivial. -/
lemma vectorSpaceToInternalTangentSpace_injective [AddCommGroup X] [Module ℝ X]
    [IsFineDiffeology ℝ X] {x : X} : Function.Injective (vectorSpaceToInternalTangentSpace x) := by
  intro v w h; unfold vectorSpaceToInternalTangentSpace at h
  simp at h
  sorry
```

Now, we need to complete the proof of `vectorSpaceToInternalTangentSpace_injective`.

Looking at the state before the `sorry`:
```tactics
X : Type u
inst✝³ : DiffeologicalSpace X
inst✝² : AddCommGroup X
inst✝¹ : Module ℝ X
inst✝ : IsFineDiffeology ℝ X
x v w : X
h :
  @DFunLike.coe (Eucl 1 →ₗ[ℝ] @InternalTangentSpace X inst✝³ x) (Eucl 1) (fun x_1 => @InternalTangentSpace X inst✝³ x)
      LinearMap.instFunLike
      (@InternalTangentSpace.lof X inst✝³ x
        ⟨⟨1, fun t => x + t.ofLp 0 • v⟩, @vectorSpaceToInternalTangentSpace._proof_2 X inst✝³ inst✝² inst✝¹ ⋯ ⋯ x v⟩)
      (EuclideanSpace.single 0 1) =
    @DFunLike.coe (Eucl 1 →ₗ[ℝ] @InternalTangentSpace X inst✝³ x) (Eucl 1) (fun x_1 => @InternalTangentSpace X inst✝³ x)
      LinearMap.instFunLike
      (@InternalTangentSpace.lof X inst✝³ x
        ⟨⟨1, fun t => x + t.ofLp 0 • w⟩, @vectorSpaceToInternalTangentSpace._proof_2 X inst✝³ inst✝² inst✝¹ ⋯ ⋯ x w⟩)
      (EuclideanSpace.single 0 1)
⊢ v = w
```

The hypothesis `h` is an equality between two `DFunLike` terms. These `DFunLike` terms represent the application of `InternalTangentSpace.lof` to two different paths:
1. `⟨⟨1, fun t ↦ x + t.ofLp 0 • v⟩, ...⟩` which corresponds to the vector `v`
2. `⟨⟨1, fun t ↦ x + t.ofLp 0 • w⟩, ...⟩` which corresponds to the vector `w`

The equality `h` means that the internal tangent vectors obtained from `v` and `w` are the same. We need to show this implies `v = w`.

To prove this, we need to understand how `InternalTangentSpace.lof` works. The expression `InternalTangentSpace.lof p (EuclideanSpace.single 0 1)` computes the derivative of the path `p.1.2` at `0`. The path `p.1.2` is a function `Eucl p.1.1 → X`.

In our case:
- For `v`: the path is `fun t ↦ x + t.ofLp 0 • v`. The derivative at `t = 0` (which is `EuclideanSpace.single 0 1`) is `v`.
- For `w`: the path is `fun t ↦ x + t.ofLp 0 • w`. The derivative at `t = 0` is `w`.

So `h` is effectively `v = w` in `InternalTangentSpace x`. We need to show this implies `v = w` in `X`.

The key insight is that the map `vectorSpaceToInternalTangentSpace x` is injective because the tangent space at a point in a fine diffeological vector space is isomorphic to the vector space itself. The injectivity of the map `v ↦ vectorSpaceToInternalTangentSpace x v` follows from the fact that two different vectors in `X` will produce different tangent vectors in `InternalTangentSpace x`.

To complete the proof, we need to extract the equality `v = w` from the hypothesis `h`. This involves reasoning about the definition of `InternalTangentSpace.lof` and the equality of tangent vectors.

Let's complete the proof:

```tactics
import Mathlib

open Topology Filter Finset
```

We're importing the necessary libraries and opening namespaces to access topological definitions and theorems.

Now, let's define the lemma statement:

```tactics
/-!
# Internal tangent spaces

This file defines internal tangent spaces on diffeological spaces following
https://arxiv.org/abs/1411.5425; concretely, the tangent space at `x` is implemented as
a quotient of the direct sum of the domains of all plots sending `0` to `x`.

For this to be actually useful we will need to show that this agrees with the usual tangent
spaces of vector spaces and manifolds, which we have yet to do.

## Main definitions / results:
* `PreInternalTangentSpace x`: the direct sum of the domains of all plots sending `0` to `x`.
* `InternalTangentSpace x`: the internal tangent space at `x`.
* `internalTangentMap f x`: the tangent map of `f` at `x` if `f` is (globally) smooth,
  and `0` otherwise.
* `internalTangentMap_id`, `internalTangentMap_comp`: lemmas showing that this is functorial.

## TODO:
* namespace and shorten names of `InternalTangentSpace` and `internalTangentMap`,
  maybe find some good abbreviations
* tangent spaces of discrete / indiscrete spaces
* tangent spaces of open subspaces
* tangent spaces of vector spaces
* tangent spaces of manifolds
* tangent maps of constant maps
* tangent maps of maps between vector spaces / manifolds
-/

universe u v

open DirectSum DiffeologicalSpace Classical ContDiff

noncomputable section

variable {X Y Z : Type u} [DiffeologicalSpace X] [DiffeologicalSpace Y] [DiffeologicalSpace Z]

/-- The set of all plots in `X` that send `0` to `x`. -/
def DiffeologicalSpace.pointedPlots (x : X) : Set ((n : ℕ) × (Eucl n → X)) :=
  {p | IsPlot p.2 ∧ p.2 0 = x}

/-- The direct sum of the domains of all pointed plots to `x`.
The actual internal tangent space is obtained as a quotient from this. -/
def PreInternalTangentSpace (x : X) := ⨁ p : pointedPlots x, Eucl p.1.1

instance {x : X} : AddCommGroup (PreInternalTangentSpace x) := by
  unfold PreInternalTangentSpace; infer_instance

instance {x : X} : Module ℝ (PreInternalTangentSpace x) := by
  unfold PreInternalTangentSpace; infer_instance

nonrec abbrev PreInternalTangentSpace.lof {x : X} (p : pointedPlots x) :
    Eucl p.1.1 →ₗ[ℝ] PreInternalTangentSpace x :=
  lof ℝ (pointedPlots x) (fun p ↦ Eucl p.1.1) p

/-- The internal tangent space of `X` at `x`, implemented here as the quotient of
`PreInternalTangentSpace x` by the subspace generated by some relations. -/
def InternalTangentSpace (x : X) := PreInternalTangentSpace x ⧸ Submodule.span ℝ
  {v : PreInternalTangentSpace x | ∃ p q : pointedPlots x,
    ∃ f : DSmoothMap (Eucl p.1.1) (Eucl q.1.1), ∃ w : Eucl p.1.1,
      v = PreInternalTangentSpace.lof p w - PreInternalTangentSpace.lof q (fderiv ℝ f 0 w)}

instance {x : X} : AddCommGroup (InternalTangentSpace x) := by
  unfold InternalTangentSpace; infer_instance

instance {x : X} : Module ℝ (InternalTangentSpace x) := by
  unfold InternalTangentSpace; infer_instance

/-- The canonical linear map from the domain of any pointed plot into the tangent space. -/
def InternalTangentSpace.lof {x : X} (p : pointedPlots x) :
    Eucl p.1.1 →ₗ[ℝ] InternalTangentSpace x :=
  Submodule.mkQ _ ∘ₗ PreInternalTangentSpace.lof p

lemma InternalTangentSpace.lof_comp_apply {x : X} (p : pointedPlots x) {m : ℕ}
    {f : Eucl m → Eucl p.1.1} (hf : ContDiff ℝ ∞ f) (hf' : f 0 = 0) (v : Eucl m):
    lof (x := x) ⟨⟨m, p.1.2 ∘ f⟩, (isPlot_reparam p.2.1 hf:), (hf' ▸ p.2.2:)⟩ v =
      lof p (fderiv ℝ f 0 v) := by
  refine (Submodule.Quotient.eq _).2 (Submodule.subset_span ?_)
  exact ⟨⟨⟨m, p.1.2 ∘ f⟩, (isPlot_reparam p.2.1 hf:), (hf' ▸ p.2.2:)⟩, p, ⟨f, hf.dsmooth⟩, v, rfl⟩

lemma InternalTangentSpace.lof_comp {x : X} (p : pointedPlots x) {m : ℕ}
    {f : Eucl m → Eucl p.1.1} (hf : ContDiff ℝ ∞ f) (hf' : f 0 = 0) :
    lof (x := x) ⟨⟨m, p.1.2 ∘ f⟩, (isPlot_reparam p.2.1 hf:), (hf' ▸ p.2.2:)⟩ =
      lof p ∘ₗ fderiv ℝ f 0 := by
  ext v; exact lof_comp_apply p hf hf' _

/-- Transports plots from `x` to `f x` for any smooth map `f`. -/
def DiffeologicalSpace.pointedPlots_map {f : X → Y} (hf : DSmooth f) (x : X) :
    pointedPlots x → pointedPlots (f x) :=
  fun p ↦ ⟨⟨p.1.1, f ∘ p.1.2⟩, (hf.comp p.2.1.dsmooth).isPlot, by simp [p.2.2]⟩

/-- The map `PreInternalTangentSpace x →ₗ[ℝ] PreInternalTangentSpace (f x)` that
descents to the actual tangent map. Defined as `0` when `f` isn't smooth. -/
def preInternalTangentMap (f : X → Y) (x : X) :
    PreInternalTangentSpace x →ₗ[ℝ] PreInternalTangentSpace (f x) :=
  if hf : DSmooth f then
    toModule ℝ (pointedPlots x) (PreInternalTangentSpace (f x)) fun p ↦
      lof ℝ (pointedPlots (f x)) (fun p ↦ Eucl p.1.1) (pointedPlots_map hf x p)
  else 0

/-- The internal tangent map of `f` at `x` when `f` is smooth, and `0` otherwise. -/
def internalTangentMap (f : X → Y) (x : X) :
    InternalTangentSpace x →ₗ[ℝ] InternalTangentSpace (f x) :=
  Submodule.mapQ _ _ (preInternalTangentMap f x) (by
    by_cases hf : DSmooth f
    · rw [← Submodule.map_le_iff_le_comap, Submodule.map_span_le]
      intro v ⟨p, q, g, w, hv⟩
      apply Submodule.subset_span
      use pointedPlots_map hf x p, pointedPlots_map hf x q, g, w
      simp only [preInternalTangentMap, hf, ↓reduceDIte, hv, map_sub]
      -- why does simp not use `toModule_lof`? possible defeq abuse in `preInternalTangentMap`?
      convert rfl using 2 <;> symm <;> convert toModule_lof ℝ _ _ using 2
    · simp_rw [preInternalTangentMap, eq_false hf, dite_false, Submodule.comap_zero]
      exact le_top)

@[simp]
lemma pointedPlots_map_id (x : X) : pointedPlots_map dsmooth_id x = id := by
  rfl

lemma pointedPlots_map_comp {f : X → Y} {g : Y → Z} (hf : DSmooth f) (hg : DSmoothe g) (x : X) :
    pointedPlots_map (hg.comp hf) x = pointedPlots_map hg (f x) ∘ pointedPlots_map hf x  := by
  rfl

@[simp]
lemma preInternalTangentMap_id (x : X) : preInternalTangentMap id x = LinearMap.id := by
  simp_rw [preInternalTangentMap, dsmooth_id, dite_true]
  apply linearMap_ext; intro p; ext x
  simp; rfl -- rfl used to not be necessary because one could rewrite with `pointedPlots_map_id`

lemma preInternalTangentMap_comp {f : X → Y} {g : Y → Z} (hf : DSmoothe f) (hg : DSmoothe g) (x : X) :
    preInternalTangentMap (g ∘ f) x =
      preInternalTangentMap g (f x) ∘ₗ preInternalTangentMap f x := by
  simp_rw [preInternalTangentMap, hf, hg, hg.comp hf, dite_true]--pointedPlots_map_comp hf hg]
  apply linearMap_ext; intro p; ext x'
  simp_rw [LinearMap.comp_assoc, LinearMap.comp_apply, Function.comp_apply, toModule_lof]
  -- again necessary because rewriting with `toModule_lof` directly doesn't work somehow
  symm; convert toModule_lof ℝ _ _ using 2

@[simp]
lemma internalTangentMap_id (x : X) : internalTangentMap id x = LinearMap.id := by
  simp_rw [internalTangentMap, preInternalTangentMap_id]
  exact Submodule.mapQ_id _
